keyPoints: The CRC-CCITT algorithm is used for error detection in digital communication systems.
It generates a 16-bit check code for each 16-bit data byte.
The check code is calculated using the polynomial x^16 + x^12 + x^5 + 1.
To detect errors, compare the received check code with the calculated check code.
If the two check codes do not match, an error has occurred.
The CRC-CCITT algorithm is widely used in various applications, including data transmission and storage.
